inputs.label: Operating Time (hours)
inputs.businessContext: Total time the system is expected to operate in a year. Typically 8760 hours (365 days * 24 hours). Based on ISO 14224.

inputs.label: Total Downtime (hours)
inputs.businessContext: Total time the system is not operational due to failures or maintenance. Includes corrective and preventive downtime.

inputs.label: Number of Failures
inputs.businessContext: Total number of failures during the operating time. Used to calculate MTBF.

inputs.label: Cost per Downtime Hour ($)
inputs.businessContext: Financial loss per hour of downtime, including lost production, labor, and penalties.

inputs.label: Maintenance Cost per Failure ($)
inputs.businessContext: Average cost of repairing a failure, including parts, labor, and logistics.

inputs.label: Data Confidence (%)
inputs.businessContext: Confidence level in the input data quality. Used to adjust results for uncertainty.

hiddenLossDrivers: availability < 0.9: Critical loss of production capacity
mtbf < 100: Frequent failures indicate poor reliability
mttr > 10: Long repair times indicate poor maintainability
suggestedActions: If availability < 0.9: Implement preventive maintenance program
If mtbf < 100: Conduct root cause analysis and redesign
If mttr > 10: Improve spare parts availability and technician training
